NCT04460885.name: ONWARDS-1
NCT04460885.group: Insulin-naive T2D, icodec once-weekly vs glargine U100 once-daily (wk 52)

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 52 (primary, icodec vs glargine, ETD)

NCT04460885.snippet: Source: Rosenstock J et al. N Engl J Med 2023;389:297-308 (ONWARDS-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 984 insulin-naive adults with type 2 diabetes on background oral agents or GLP-1 RA randomized 1:1 open-label to insulin icodec once weekly (n=492) or insulin glargine U100 once daily (n=492) for 52 weeks plus 26-week extension. Open-label because weekly vs daily injection frequency cannot be blinded.

evidence.label: Primary - HbA1c Change at Week 52
evidence.text: HbA1c change from baseline at week 52: icodec -1.55% vs glargine -1.35%; estimated treatment difference -0.19 pct points (95% CI -0.36 to -0.03; P<0.001 for non-inferiority and superiority). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Hypoglycaemia and Weight
evidence.text: Rate of combined clinically significant (<3.0 mmol/L) or severe hypoglycaemia: icodec 0.30 vs glargine 0.16 events per patient-year-exposure (RR 1.64, non-significant). Body weight change icodec +1.0 kg vs glargine +0.2 kg (ETD 0.75 kg).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label by necessity (weekly SC vs daily SC injection frequency). D3 LOW - 95% completion. D4 LOW - HbA1c central lab (blinded). D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female aged above or equal to 18 years at the time of signing informed consent.~* Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2D) 180 days or more prior to the day of screening.~* HbA1c from 7.0-11.0% (53.0-96.7 mmol/mol) both inclusive at screening confirmed by central laboratory analysis.~* Insulin naïve. However, short term insulin treatment for a maximum of 14 days prior to the day of screening is allowed, as is prior insulin treatment for gestational diabetes.~*...

NCT04770532.name: ONWARDS-2
NCT04770532.group: T2D switching from once-daily basal insulin, icodec vs degludec (wk 26)

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 26 (primary, icodec vs degludec, ETD)

NCT04770532.snippet: Source: Philis-Tsimikas A et al. Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol 2023;11:414-425 (ONWARDS-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 526 adults with type 2 diabetes previously treated with once-daily basal insulin randomized 1:1 open-label to switch to insulin icodec once weekly (n=263) or continue insulin degludec once daily (n=263) for 26 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - HbA1c Change at Week 26
evidence.text: HbA1c change from baseline at week 26: icodec -0.93% vs degludec -0.71%; estimated treatment difference -0.22 pct points (95% CI -0.37 to -0.08; P=0.0028 for superiority). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - TIR and Hypoglycaemia
evidence.text: Time in range (70-180 mg/dL) at wk 22-26: icodec 63.1% vs degludec 59.5%; ETD 3.6 pct points. Combined clinically significant + severe hypoglycaemia rates similar between arms.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label. D3 LOW - 96% completion. D4 LOW - central HbA1c. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female aged above or equal to 18 years at the time of signing informed consent.~* Diagnosed with T2D greater than or equal to 180 days prior to the day of screening.~* HbA1c from 7.0-10.0% (53.0 85.8 mmol/mol) both inclusive at screening confirmed by central laboratory analysis.~* Treated with once daily or twice daily basal insulin (Neutral Protamine Hagedorn insulin, insulin degludec, insulin detemir, insulin glargine 100 units/mL, or insulin glargine 300 units/m...

NCT04795531.name: ONWARDS-3

allOutcomes.title: HbA1c change at week 26 (primary, icodec vs degludec, ETD)

NCT04795531.snippet: Source: Lingvay I et al. JAMA 2023;330:228-237 (ONWARDS-3).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 588 insulin-naive adults with type 2 diabetes randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to insulin icodec once weekly (n=294) or insulin degludec once daily (n=294) for 26 weeks. Double-dummy design used to maintain blinding across weekly and daily injection schedules.

evidence.label: Primary - HbA1c Change at Week 26
evidence.text: HbA1c change from baseline at week 26: icodec -1.57% vs degludec -1.36%; estimated treatment difference -0.21 pct points (95% CI -0.34 to -0.08; P<0.001 for non-inferiority and superiority). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Patient-Reported Treatment Satisfaction
evidence.text: Diabetes Treatment Satisfaction Questionnaire change score favoured icodec (DTSQs-change 4.22 vs 2.96 in degludec; P<0.001). Clinically significant + severe hypoglycaemia rates 0.76 vs 0.27 per PYE (icodec vs degludec; P=0.02).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind double-dummy (unusual for insulin-vs-insulin comparator; robust masking). D3 LOW - 96% completion. D4 LOW - central HbA1c. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female aged above or equal to 18 years at the time of signing informed consent.~* Diagnosed with T2D (type 2 diabetes) greater than or equal to 180 days prior to the day of screening.~* HbA1c (glycated haemoglobin) from 7.0-11.0% (53.0-96.7 mmol/mol) both inclusive at screening confirmed by central laboratory analysis.~* Insulin naïve. However, short term insulin treatment for a maximum of 14 days prior to the day of screening is allowed, as is prior insulin treatm...

title: RapidMeta Endocrinology | Insulin Icodec Once-Weekly Basal Insulin in Type 2 Diabetes v1.1
h1: Insulin Icodec Once-Weekly Basal Insulin \u2014 Patient Summary
